summary: Round 1 findings 1 and 2 are resolved with tight, directional assertions; finding 3 is deferred on sound grounds. No regressions: every Round 1 assertion is preserved — both fixes are purely additive. Test count is stable and matches the commit's claim (30 :app:testDebugUnitTest cases, +1 vs Round 1). Litmus test for the two tightened cases: if production regressed to `CRASH_TRUNC_MARKER + head` (marker at start), `crashContent.endsWith("[truncated at 100 KB]")` would fail; if the `exitCode == null` branch in LogcatReader were removed or the literal `[logcat unavailable: unknown]` retexted, `nullExitNoTimeout_returnsUnavailableUnknown` would fail. Both tightened tests now kill the mutation they target. Finding 3 deferral is accepted — CrashReport bypasses logcat by Decision 8, so the non-Hilt DefaultCommandRunner path is not runtime-load-bearing for the secondary ctor; the existing eager-instantiation guardrail catches `internal` or deleted-constructor regressions at compile time, and the Hilt path runtime is already covered by `aboutSource_logcatPopulatedFromRunner`. No outstanding findings.

verification.finding_1_evidence: LogExportManagerTest.kt:184-188 — crashLogHeadTruncation_preservesHeadAndMarks now strips the section header via `crashSection.removePrefix("=== crash.log ===").trim()` and asserts `crashContent.endsWith("[truncated at 100 KB]")`. Directional anchor symmetric with the logcat tail test (LogExportManagerTest.kt:154, which anchors the marker in the first 200 chars). The Round 1 head-marker `contains(headMarker)` assertion and the 100KB+128 byte-ceiling assertion are preserved. A regression placing the marker at the start of the section (e.g. `CRASH_TRUNC_MARKER + head`) would now fail `.endsWith`, whereas Round 1 assertions would have stayed green.
verification.finding_2_evidence: LogcatReaderTest.kt:45-56 — new test `nullExitNoTimeout_returnsUnavailableUnknown` feeds `StubCommandRunner(LogcatResult(stdout = ByteArray(0), exitCode = null, timedOut = false))` and asserts `reader.read() == "[logcat unavailable: unknown]"`. This is the exact state DefaultCommandRunner produces after catching IOException/SecurityException from ProcessBuilder.start (confirmed in the inline comment at the test and matches LogcatReader.kt:42 branch order `timedOut → exitCode==null → exitCode!=0 → isEmpty`). Locks both the branch existence and the placeholder string. Removing the `exitCode == null` branch (or having it fall through to the exit=N branch) would produce a different string and fail this test.
verification.finding_3_rationale: Acceptable. The nonHiltConstruction_instantiatesWithoutInjection test is explicitly a compile-time guardrail: eager construction of LogcatReader(DefaultCommandRunner()) in the secondary ctor means any `internal`-downgrade or deleted-constructor regression fails to compile. Runtime non-Hilt logcat coverage is not load-bearing because (a) CrashReport bypasses logcat by Decision 8, which is the only source the secondary ctor is documented to serve, and (b) the Hilt path's runtime behaviour through DefaultCommandRunner is structurally equivalent and already covered by `aboutSource_logcatPopulatedFromRunner` under the primary ctor. The suggested Option (b) twin would exercise Robolectric's ProcessBuilder which returns exit=nonzero or empty reliably — marginal value for a low-severity finding, adds Robolectric surface area to an already-Robolectric test, and the value is bounded by what the stub path already proves.
verification.test_count_detail: Round 1 total: 29 @Test methods. Round 2 total: 30 @Test methods (+1 `nullExitNoTimeout_returnsUnavailableUnknown` in LogcatReaderTest). Breakdown: TapCounterTest=6, LogcatReaderTest=6 (was 5, +1 for Finding 2), DeviceInfoCollectorTest=3, LogExportManagerTest=15 (unchanged — the Round 1 fix modified assertions inside crashLogHeadTruncation_preservesHeadAndMarks without adding a new @Test). Matches the commit message's "30 тестов (+1), все зелёные" claim exactly.
verification.regressions_detail: No assertion was removed or loosened. Finding 1 fix is purely additive: the original `crashSection.contains("[truncated at 100 KB]")` was replaced with a strictly stronger `crashContent.endsWith("[truncated at 100 KB]")` on the header-stripped content — endsWith implies contains, so anything the old assertion caught, the new one still catches, plus the directional anchor. The 100KB+128 byte-ceiling assertion and the head-marker contains assertion are unchanged. Finding 2 fix is a new @Test with no impact on existing tests. The DeviceInfoCollector.kt Locale import change (Round 1 code-reviewer finding #6) is cosmetic and has no test impact.
